title: ulmus minor subsp. minor
intro: Ulmus minor subsp. minor, also known as the dwarf or field elm, is a subspecies of the European white elm tree (Ulmus minor). It is a deciduous tree with a broad, vase-shaped crown and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal medicine. The bark of the Ulmus minor subsp. minor contains a compound called salicylic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bark can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, offering potential health benefits including reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and relief from colds and flu.
